channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: 29th Sunday after Pentecost
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: 29th Sunday after Pentecost

item.title: 29th Sunday after Pentecost, The Healing Of The 10 Lepers, 10 Are Cleansed But Only The One With Graditude Is Made Whole, 2007-12-03
item.description: :29th Sunday after Pentecost. The Healing Of The 10 Lepers, 10 Are Cleansed But Only The One With Graditude Is Made Whole, 2007-12-03
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :29th Sunday after Pentecost. The Healing Of The 10 Lepers, 10 Are Cleansed But Only The One With Graditude Is Made Whole, 2007-12-03

item.title: 29th Sunday after Pentecost, The Healing Of The 10 Lepers, Arise, go thy way thy faith hath made thee whole., 2009-12-20
item.description: :29th Sunday after Pentecost. The Healing Of The 10 Lepers, Arise, go thy way thy faith hath made thee whole., 2009-12-20
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :29th Sunday after Pentecost. The Healing Of The 10 Lepers, Arise, go thy way thy faith hath made thee whole., 2009-12-20

item.title: 29th Sunday after Pentecost, 2012-12-23
item.description: :29th Sunday after Pentecost. The response of gratitude of the one leper of the ten who were healed by Jesus prompted our Lord to say to him " thy faith hath made thee whole." This common expression of Jesus must be investigated closely, for in each instance, different aspects of faith are revealed. This Samaritan's faith was expressed in two ways, that he prayed for mercy and he gave thanks for his great gift. Regarding his prayer, we have a detailed interlude to talk about the Jesus prayer and how we should pray it, including praying it for others. Then we link the prayer to thanksgiving, for without the latter, the former withers. As we become aware of how much God's mercy surrounds us and helps us. we thank Him, and seek his mercy even more, for ourselves and others. Our heart becomes softer, and feels the needs of others - our heart becomes like that of Jesus. These two things, seeking God's mercy and thanking him for everything - are required for salvation.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :29th Sunday after Pentecost. The response of gratitude of the one leper of the ten who were healed by Jesus prompted our Lord to say to him " thy faith hath made thee whole." This common expression of Jesus must be investigated closely, for in each instance, different aspects of faith are revealed. This Samaritan's faith was expressed in two ways, that he prayed for mercy and he gave thanks for his great gift. Regarding his prayer, we have a detailed interlude to talk about the Jesus prayer and how we should pray it, including praying it for others. Then we link the prayer to thanksgiving, for without the latter, the former withers. As we become aware of how much God's mercy surrounds us and helps us. we thank Him, and seek his mercy even more, for ourselves and others. Our heart becomes softer, and feels the needs of others - our heart becomes like that of Jesus. These two things, seeking God's mercy and thanking him for everything - are required for salvation.

item.title: 29th Sunday after Pentecost, 2013-12-22
item.description: :29th Sunday after Pentecost. There is great power and energy in gratitude. It is fundamental part of the Christian life, and absolutely necessity if we are to "be made whole" - and this is the aim and purpose of human life. We look at the two healings in the healing of the ten lepers. All were healed of leprosy but only one was made whole. Let us learn how to inculcate gratitude in practical ways in our life. We also speak briefly on the Jesus prayer and how it should be a model for our prayer.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :29th Sunday after Pentecost. There is great power and energy in gratitude. It is fundamental part of the Christian life, and absolutely necessity if we are to "be made whole" - and this is the aim and purpose of human life. We look at the two healings in the healing of the ten lepers. All were healed of leprosy but only one was made whole. Let us learn how to inculcate gratitude in practical ways in our life. We also speak briefly on the Jesus prayer and how it should be a model for our prayer.
